paper_title: Analysis of Cell Cycle Phase and DNA Damage

methods.method_name: Immunofluorescence Analysis of Cell Cycle Phase and DNA Damage
methods.description: This method involves the use of immunofluorescence to observe the cell cycle phase in PMII and the distribution of phosphorylated histone H2AX (gH2AX) in generative and sperm cells. It is used to study cell cycle dynamics and DNA damage.

steps.description: After 12 or 24 h of culture, the generative cells or sperm cells in the pollen tubes were isolated and simultaneously fixed for 30 min in fixative containing 4 % (w/v) paraformaldehyde, 0.1 % (v/v) glutaraldehyde and 2 % (v/v) polyoxyethylene (20) sorbitan monolaurate in microtubule-stabilizing buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: Fix cells with paraformaldehyde and glutaraldehyde in microtubule-stabilizing buffer

steps.description: The cells were then transferred to a polylysine-coated coverslip using a microcapillary connected to a micropump.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer fixed cells to polylysine-coated coverslip using microcapillary

steps.description: The fixed cells were treated with a blocking agent for 60 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Block non-specific binding sites on cells

steps.description: Cells were incubated with antibodies in phosphate-buffered saline supplemented with 0.05 % bovine serum albumin for 60 min at 37°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate cells with antibodies in PBS with BSA

steps.description: For gH2AX detection, use anti-A. thaliana gH2AX rabbit polyclonal antibody and Alexa Fluor 488 goat anti-rabbit antibody.
For microtubule staining, use anti-a-tubulin mouse monoclonal antibody and Alexa Fluor 546 goat anti-mouse antibody.
Cells were stained with DAPI for 15 min and mounted on coverslips in an antifade reagent.
steps.searchable_summary: Stain cells with DAPI and mount in antifade reagent

steps.description: Images were taken in 1.0-mm steps along the Z-axis using a laser-scanning confocal microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture Z-stack images with confocal microscope

steps.description: Each image stack was combined into a single image using Olympus FV10-ASW Ver. 3.1a.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine Z-stack images into single image
